mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 100k jobs per month signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 100k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal fails to outperform baseline in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in both GDP growth and housing starts indicates weakening demand conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining GDPC1 and HOUST are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the combined signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% triggers central bank rate hikes, creating headwinds for equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 2.0 are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining INDPRO are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal fails to outperform baseline in 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates strong demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising RSAFS and falling UNRATE are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in unemployment rate above 5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 5.0 are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal fails to outperform baseline in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production alongside declining manufacturing inventories signals strong supply-side dynamics, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising INDPRO and HOUST are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03).
